items.title: Lectric Memorial Day Sale offers up to $694 in FREE gear + up to $500 price cuts on e-bikes starting from $799
items.summary:
Lectric has just launched its Memorial Day e-bike Sale, one of the brand’s biggest sale events of the year, not to mention of summer, and you’re able to score up to $720 total savings: up to $694 in FREE bundled gear alongside two models getting up to $500 price drops. Many of the brand’s popular e-bikes are getting increased bundle packages during this event, like my personal pick for all-around commuting versatility, the XP4 750 Folding Utility e-bike that comes with $473 in FREE gear at $1,299 shipped, while the standard XP4 e-bike is getting a FREE $227 bundle at $999 shipped. Without the savings on the bundles, these packages would normally run you $1,772 and $1,226, respectively. The standard XP4 bundles contain the usual LevelUp rack, suspension seat post, and accordion bike lock, while the XP4 750 e-bike gets those alongside an Elite headlight, a phone mount, a pair of mirrors, and a water-resistant pannier bag. Head below to learn more (or check out our hands-on review here) and browse the full lineup of Memorial Day deals.

items.title: Everything Google announced at its Android Show, from Googlebooks to vibe-coded widgets

items.title: Google's Android-powered laptops are called Googlebooks, and they're coming this year
items.summary: Google has revealed its vision for the AI laptop of tomorrow.

items.title: Android is getting a big AI overhaul in 2026
items.summary: Google has big plans for Android in 2026, and most of it is AI.

items.title: The AI legal services industry is heating up — Anthropic is getting in on the action
items.summary: Anthropic's new tools are designed to help law firms automate specific clerical functions — things like document search and review, case law resources, deposition prep, document drafting, and other related areas.

items.title: Google adds Gemini-powered dictation to Gboard, which could be bad news for dictation startups
items.summary: Google's transcription feature will initially launch with Samsung Galaxy and Google Pixel phones.

items.title: Android adds a feature to stop you from doomscrolling
items.summary: Google’s new Android feature, Pause Point, forces you to wait before opening distracting apps in an effort to curb addictive scrolling habits.

items.title: Google unveils Googlebook, a new line of AI-native laptops
items.summary: The company says Googlebooks, which are launching this fall, are the first laptops designed from the ground up for Gemini Intelligence to offer personal and proactive help.

items.title: Google’s ‘Create My Widget’ feature will let you vibe-code your own widgets
items.summary: To create a widget, users will be able to describe what they want using natural language. For example, you could ask the feature to "suggest three high-protein meal prep recipes every week" in order to get a custom dashboard that you can add and resize on your home screen.

items.title: Google brings agentic AI and vibe-coded widgets to Android
items.summary: Gemini Intelligence will also include Gboard-based dictation and form-filling capabilities.

items.title: Google launches new Android security feature to help uncover spyware attacks
items.summary: Intrusion Logging is a new part of Android’s Advanced Protection Mode, which aims to help protect human rights activists, journalists, and dissidents from government spyware attack and law enforcement forensic devices.

items.title: Android Auto is now one (screen) size fits all
items.summary: This week at I/O, Google rolled out a series of major updates for Android Auto that include better sizing for unconventionally shaped screens, video streaming through YouTube, widget support, and of course, more AI-powered Gemini features. The company also announced some updates for cars that run on Google's built-in native software, including the ability to […]

items.title: Google announces its Chromebook successor: the Googlebook
items.summary: Google is announcing a new line of laptops coming in the fall called Googlebooks. Details are sparse for now, as the tease is just a small part of various Android announcements during Google's Android Show. But we do know this is a major new initiative in the laptop space for Google, seemingly designed to succeed […]

items.title: The 9 biggest new features in Android 17
items.summary: Would it shock you to hear that Android 17 is filled with new AI-enabled features, like improved dictation and vibe-coded widgets? Fortunately, that's not all. The platform is getting non-AI updates too, from an emoji overhaul to a new screentime tool that helps you avoid distracting apps. Google has just revealed the biggest changes coming […]

items.title: Gemini’s latest updates are all about controlling your phone
items.summary: It is, once again, Gemini season. Google is announcing a host of new Gemini features during its pre-I/O Android showcase, many of which aim to help use your phone for you. You'll find Gemini in more places, like Chrome on Android, in your autofill suggestions, and all up in your apps - if you want. […]

items.title: Hamas 'weaponised' sexual violence in 7 October attacks, Israeli investigation says
items.summary: The most comprehensive report of its kind details harrowing cases of sexual attacks which it says were systematic.

items.title: Indigo brings the open social web to one app
items.summary: Indigo's new social app lets you cross-post to the open social web, including Mastodon and Bluesky, and browse a unified timeline.

items.title: South Africa's top court bars repeat asylum applications
items.summary: The home affairs minister hails the ruling as a "victory" against the "abuse" of the refugee system.

items.title: US bank discloses security lapse after sharing customer data with AI app
items.summary: Community Bank, which operates in Pennsylvania, Ohio, and West Virginia, disclosed a cybersecurity incident that exposed customers’ names, dates of birth, and Social Security numbers.

items.title: Threads tests a Meta AI integration that works similarly to Grok
items.summary: The feature is designed to help people get real-time context about trends and breaking stories, as well as receive recommendations, all within conversations.

items.title: Zelensky's ex-chief of staff in court as Ukraine corruption probe escalates
items.summary: Andriy Yermak was named by Ukraine's two anti-corruption agencies as a suspect in a money-laundering scheme.

items.title: Exaforce raises $125M Series B to build AI for catching and stopping cyberattacks as they happen
items.summary: As bad actors weaponize AI to exploit software vulnerabilities at unprecedented speed, companies are increasingly recognizing the need to bolster their cybersecurity defenses. The round valued the three-year-old startup at $725 million.

items.title: Uganda's president sworn in for record seventh term
items.summary: Yoweri Museveni, 81, won disputed elections in January to extend his 40-year rule.

items.title: Waymo issues recall to deal with a flooding problem
items.summary: Waymo has issued a software recall that makes its robotaxis more cautious around flooded areas. A "final remedy" is in the works.

items.title: Paydibs strengthens DuitNow QR support with direct PayNet integration
items.summary: Paydibs has announced that it is now a direct participant of Payments Network Malaysia (PayNet) for DuitNow QR acceptance. Previously, it has offered DuitNow QR acceptance to merchants nationwide through third-party arrangements with strategic partners. However, this latest direct integration with PayNet marks a significant infrastructure upgrade, allowing Paydibs to connect directly to the national […]

items.title: eBay rejects $55.5bn offer from GameStop
items.summary: The online auction giant said it doubted how the video game retailer would finance its offer.

items.title: MITI’s EV policy flip-flop raises bigger questions than just protecting Proton | Let’s Talk About #143
items.summary: Malaysia’s EV industry is facing major uncertainty again following MITI’s latest policy changes for fully imported (CBU) electric vehicles. Starting 1st July 2026, imported EVs will need to meet stricter requirements including a minimum RM200,000 CIF value and at least 180kW (241hp) of power output. In reality, this effectively pushes the selling price floor to […]

items.title: Amazon employees are "tokenmaxxing" due to pressure to use AI tools
items.summary: Workers are using an internal AI tool to automate non-essential tasks.

items.title: Mexico cancels plans to end school year early for World Cup
items.summary: Parents were angry at a proposal to end classes on 5 June instead of 15 July due to the tournament and expected extreme heat.

items.title: Canvas hack: Company pays criminals to delete students' stolen data
